about.metaDescription: BEATRIP is a flight-deal portal for travel in and out of Japan. We automatically aggregate airline sales and surface flash deals, sale-timing forecasts, and price history in one place.
about.title: About BEATRIP
about.intro: BEATRIP is a flight-deal portal built around a single promise: never miss the lowest fare. We continuously aggregate and analyze airline sales across Japan.

features.title: Automated sale tracking
features.description: We pull sale data from major Japanese carriers including ANA, JAL, Peach, and Jetstar — so you don't have to check ten airline sites yourself.

features.title: Sale-timing forecasts
features.description: Our model analyzes historical sale patterns to predict when the next sale is most likely, helping you book at the right moment.

features.title: Price alerts
features.description: Set a target price on any route you're watching, and we'll ping you the instant a fare drops below it.

features.title: Trustworthy data
features.description: Every listing is sourced directly from airline official websites, so the fares and sale dates you see are accurate and current.

mission: Airline sale information is scattered across dozens of carrier sites and social posts, and most of it is easy to miss. BEATRIP brings it all into one place and pairs it with data-driven forecasts, so you can book flights at the cheapest possible moment.
We do not sell tickets — we're an information-only service. Always confirm details on the airline's official website before completing a booking.
about.dataSourcesIntro: Sale information is collected from the official websites of the following airlines:

faq.metaDescription: Common questions about using BEATRIP — how our sale data works, how to set up price alerts, and more.
faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Frequently asked questions

items.q: What is BEATRIP?
items.a: BEATRIP is a flight-deal site that automatically aggregates airline sales and surfaces flash deals, sale-timing forecasts, and price history. We do not sell tickets — the site is informational only.

items.q: Where does the sale data come from?
items.a: We pull sale data directly from the official websites of major carriers including ANA, JAL, Peach, Jetstar Japan, and Scoot. Listings refresh regularly, but always confirm the current fare and seat availability on the airline's official site.

items.q: Are the prices on BEATRIP accurate?
items.a: Prices are accurate as of when we fetched them. Airfares fluctuate constantly based on seat availability and exchange rates, so the real-time price may differ. Always confirm the latest fare on the airline's official site before booking.

items.q: How does the Sale Forecast work?
items.a: Our model analyzes historical sale records — start dates, frequency, discount depth, and more — to predict when the next sale is most likely. Forecasts are guidance, not a guarantee that a sale will run.

items.q: How do I set up a price alert?
items.a: On any deal's detail page, use the Price Alert section to set the price you'd like to be notified at. We'll alert you when the fare drops to or below that amount. Browser notifications are currently supported.

items.q: How do favorites work?
items.a: Tap the heart icon on any deal card to save it. Favorites live in your browser's local storage, so they'll be there the next time you visit from the same browser.

items.q: Can I book flights through BEATRIP?
items.a: No — we don't sell tickets. The Book button takes you to the airline's official site or a partner booking site, where you complete the booking.

items.q: Does BEATRIP work on mobile?
items.a: Yes. BEATRIP is a PWA (Progressive Web App), so you can add it to your phone's home screen and use it like a native app. Pick 'Add to Home Screen' in your browser menu.

items.q: Is there a dark mode?
items.a: Yes. Tap the sun/moon icon at the top right of the header to switch between light and dark mode. Your preference is saved in your browser.

items.q: Can I book hotels on BEATRIP?
items.a: No. BEATRIP does not handle hotel bookings directly. From our hotel and OTA-comparison pages, the booking buttons take you to partner sites such as Booking.com, Agoda, and Trip.com, where you complete the reservation.

items.q: Which booking site (OTA) is the cheapest?
items.a: There is no single site that is always cheapest. The lowest price shifts depending on dates, room type, membership tier, and sale timing. Compare the total price (taxes and fees included) across several sites before booking. Our per-city OTA comparison articles can help.

items.q: How do payments work?
items.a: BEATRIP does not process payments. Accepted methods (credit card, pay-at-property, etc.) and brands depend on the airline or booking site you are redirected to. Always check the available payment options and any fees on the booking screen.

items.q: Where do I cancel or change a reservation?
items.a: Cancellations and changes are handled by the airline or booking site where you made the reservation. BEATRIP does not hold your booking, so it cannot be cancelled from our site. Always check the cancellation terms (free cancellation vs. non-refundable rate) before booking.

items.q: Does BEATRIP show affiliate advertising?
items.a: Yes. Some links on BEATRIP are affiliate links. We may receive a referral fee when a booking is completed through a link, but this does not increase the price you pay. See our advertising and affiliate disclosure page for details.

items.q: Is the display order determined by advertising fees?
items.a: Listing order reflects a combination of our editorial assessment and commission rates, but prices and terms must always be confirmed on the linked site. We avoid one-sided endorsements and aim for neutral comparisons.

items.q: Are the hotel and flight prices shown accurate?
items.a: Displayed prices are indicative as of the time they were retrieved and constantly change with availability, exchange rates, and sales. Confirm the actual price and inventory on the final booking screen (total including taxes and fees).

items.q: How is data accuracy maintained?
items.a: Sale information and hotel data are updated automatically on a regular basis, but we cannot fully guarantee accuracy or timeliness. Always verify critical details (fares, cancellation terms, entry requirements) on official sites or public authorities.

items.q: Is there information about miles and award tickets?
items.a: Yes. We provide a complete guide to earning and using miles, as well as an award-ticket booking guide, and we are continually expanding our beginner-friendly travel guides.

items.q: Can I research travel insurance and eSIMs too?
items.a: Yes. We offer a travel insurance comparison page and an eSIM comparison page, plus guide articles explaining how to set them up. Use them together with our pre-trip checklist.

items.q: What if I do not understand a travel term?
items.a: We have a travel glossary covering aviation, hotel, and booking terms such as LCC, codeshare, open-jaw, OTA, and no-show, explained in plain language for first-time travelers.

items.q: Is there an English version?
items.a: Yes. Use the language switcher in the header to switch to the English version (/en). Major pages support both Japanese and English.

terms.metaDescription: BEATRIP's Terms of Service. Please read them before using the service.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.updated: Terms of Service — Last updated: May 15, 2026

sections.title: Article 1 (Application)
sections.content: These Terms set out the conditions for using BEATRIP (the "Service"). Users shall use the Service in agreement with these Terms.

sections.title: Article 2 (Service Content)
sections.content: The Service automatically collects airline sale information and provides flash deals, sale-timing forecasts, price-history data, and similar information. The Service does not sell tickets or act as a booking agent; it is for informational purposes only.

sections.title: Article 3 (Accuracy of Information)
sections.content: Information on the Service is collected from airline official websites and similar sources, but its accuracy, completeness, and timeliness are not guaranteed. Airfares and seat availability constantly change, so always confirm the latest information on the airline's official site before booking.

sections.title: Article 4 (Disclaimer)
sections.content: We bear no responsibility for any flight bookings or purchases made by users based on information on the Service. Sale-forecast results are reference information only and do not guarantee that a sale will actually occur.

sections.title: Article 5 (Prohibited Acts)
sections.content:
Users must not do any of the following when using the Service:
- Reproduce or republish the Service's content without permission
- Automated access such as scraping or crawling the Service
- Interfere with the operation of the Service
- Any other act we deem inappropriate

sections.title: Article 6 (Intellectual Property)
sections.content: Intellectual property rights related to the Service belong to us or the rightful owners. Users may not reproduce, republish, or modify the Service's content without our prior written consent.

sections.title: Article 7 (Changes / Suspension)
sections.content: We may change the content of the Service, or suspend or terminate its provision, without prior notice to users.

sections.title: Article 8 (Changes to Terms)
sections.content: We may change these Terms as necessary. The revised Terms take effect from the time they are posted on the Service.

privacy.metaDescription: BEATRIP's Privacy Policy, explaining how personal information is handled.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
sections.content:
The Service may collect the following information:
- Access environment information such as browser type and OS
- Access logs such as access date/time and pages viewed
- Information entered by users when setting price alerts
- User settings such as theme and favorites stored via cookies

sections.title: 2. Purpose of Use
sections.content:
Collected information is used for the following purposes:
- Providing, operating, and improving the Service
- Improving user experience (retaining theme settings, favorites, etc.)
- Providing the price-alert feature
- Service improvement through access analysis
- Preventing unauthorized access

sections.title: 3. Information Storage
sections.content: User settings such as favorites, price alerts, and theme are stored in your browser's local storage. This information is stored only on the user's device and is not sent to our servers.

sections.title: 4. Provision to Third Parties
sections.content: We do not provide collected information to third parties without the user's consent, except as required by law.

sections.title: 5. Access Analysis Tools
sections.content: The Service may use services such as Google Analytics for access analysis. These services use cookies to collect access information but do not include personally identifiable information. See each service's privacy policy for details.

sections.title: 6. Affiliate Links
sections.content: The Service may contain affiliate links to airline official sites or partner booking sites. If a user makes a booking via these links, we may receive a referral commission.

sections.title: 7. Use of Cookies
sections.content: The Service uses cookies to improve user experience. You can restrict cookie use via your browser settings, but some features may not work properly.

sections.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may change this Privacy Policy as necessary. The revised policy takes effect from the time it is posted on the Service.

sections.title: 9. Contact
sections.content: For inquiries about this policy, please contact us via the contact form on the site.

disclosure.metaDescription: BEATRIP uses affiliate advertising (TravelPayouts marker=729387 and others). This page lists our partner programs, how the affiliate model works, and our editorial independence.
disclosure.title: Advertising Disclosure

sections.title: 1. About affiliate advertising
sections.content: BEATRIP (the "Service") participates in performance-based affiliate programs. Outbound links on this Service (flight, hotel, eSIM, airport transfer, travel insurance booking buttons, etc. opened in a new tab) may be advertising. If you make a booking through one of these links, the Service may receive a referral commission from the advertiser. Per Japanese advertising regulations, such links carry rel="sponsored".

sections.title: 2. Affiliate programs we use
sections.content:
The Service currently uses the following partner programs (subject to change based on approval status):

● TravelPayouts (marker ID: 729387) — flight, hotel and travel-companion affiliate network
● Hotellook — cross-OTA hotel price comparison
● Booking.com — hotel bookings
● Trip.com — flights and hotels
● Agoda — hotels (strong in Asia)
● Aviasales / Skyscanner — flight metasearch
● Airalo — travel eSIM
● KiwiTaxi — airport transfers
● Travel insurance (TBD after approval)

Referral commissions are paid by the advertiser and never marked up to the user's price.

sections.title: 3. About displayed prices and availability
sections.content: The Service does not sell flights directly; it aggregates publicly available information from airlines and OTAs. Listed prices and seat availability reflect the moment of fetch. Always confirm the latest on the destination site before booking.

sections.title: 4. Editorial independence
sections.content: Sale listings, articles, and curated hotel picks are chosen for their value to users, independent of the size of any affiliate commission. "Best time to book" and seasonal forecasts are statistical guidance based on historical data, not a guarantee of any particular advertiser's sale.

sections.title: 5. Cookies and analytics
sections.content: Clicks on affiliate links are measured via Google Analytics 4 events (partner_click / hotel_click / affiliate_click). This data is used to measure conversion and improve the Service, and does not include personally identifiable information. See the Privacy Policy for details.

sections.title: 6. Changes
sections.content: This disclosure will be updated as partner programs are added or ended. The "last updated" date above reflects the most recent revision.
